summary: First test run of Meta Go IDaaS. Backend: 21/21 pytest tests passed against all required endpoints (health, SIWE nonce/verify, user sync, ZK verify-proof, relay rate-limit + nullifier replay, credentials, DID methods/resolve/cross-chain, billing plans/checkout/subscription, SBTs). Frontend: 9/10 pages render correctly (landing, dashboard with IdentityRadar, signup wizard step 0, vault with VC import modal, recovery, security, billing with 4 plans, sbt-gallery, settings). One critical routing bug: /api-docs returns 404 because the Kubernetes ingress treats any path starting with /api as backend traffic, so the Next.js /api-docs page is unreachable.

ui_bugs.issue: Route /api-docs returns backend 404 ({'detail':'Not Found'}). The K8s ingress routes everything starting with /api/* to backend:8001, so the Next.js page at /app/frontend/src/app/api-docs/page.tsx is shadowed. Page is completely unreachable in the preview environment.

action_items: Rename frontend route /api-docs to something that doesn't collide with the /api ingress prefix (e.g., /docs, /developer-docs, or /reference). Update any nav links pointing to /api-docs accordingly.

Recurring 404 console errors on every page are caused by the same /api-docs probe (or a missing favicon/asset). Verify no static asset is being requested under /api/*; if it is, move it under /public.

Optional: backend /api/auth/verify accepts any signature with len>=60 by extracting the address from the message body when siwe-py fails — acceptable in simulation mode, but log a clear WARN so production deploys don't silently skip verification.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py at 552 lines is on the upper bound of the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers (auth.py, did.py, billing.py, zk.py) before adding more endpoints.
_relay_buckets is an in-process dict — rate limiting will not be enforced across multiple backend workers/pods. For production this should move to Redis.
DID resolver does not validate checksum-case of addresses (blockchainAccountId returns lowercased hex). EIP-55 checksum would be ideal for W3C compliance.
billing/checkout for 'starter'/'pro' returns a placeholder Stripe URL (https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_...) — clearly MOCKED, ensure UI shows this as simulation before going live.
Smart contract mint and ZK Groth16 proof generation run in SIMULATION mode (no Hardhat node, contract addrs are 0x0). This is documented and expected for the preview env.
test_credentials: Not applicable — wallet-based auth in simulation mode. Backend /api/auth/verify accepts any signature with length>=60 and extracts address from the message body when siwe-py fails (graceful fallback). No real Stripe keys; no real chain RPC.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests use random 0x addresses + TEST_ prefixed handles; data persists in mongo `test_database` (idempotent upserts). No cleanup teardown was added — collections will accumulate test users/nullifiers across runs. Recommend adding a cleanup endpoint or fixture if this becomes noisy.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is 100% green and stable. The only remaining bug is the /api-docs route collision with the K8s /api ingress. After main agent renames the route, retest by hitting the new URL and verifying the api endpoint list renders. MetaMask wallet connection cannot be tested in headless browser — wizard testing is limited to step 0 (handle/consent). Mocked items: ZK proofs (simulation), smart contracts (0x0 addrs), Stripe (placeholder URL), SIWE verification (fallback to address extraction). Re-run backend_test.py with `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` for regression.
rca_of_issue: Bug: /api-docs returns 404. RCA: Kubernetes ingress configuration matches the prefix /api and forwards to backend port 8001. The path /api-docs starts with /api so the ingress routes it to FastAPI, which has no matching route and returns {'detail':'Not Found'}. The Next.js page at /app/frontend/src/app/api-docs/page.tsx is never reached. Reproduction: curl https://soulbound-identity.preview.emergentagent.com/api-docs returns 404 JSON from FastAPI. Mitigation: rename the Next.js route directory to /docs or /developer (anything not starting with /api). Update all internal links (DOCS button in header) to the new path.
